mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence weakens.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts signal weakening supply-side activity, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns as recession risks rise.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the combined INDPRO-HOUST signal falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth above 2% annually signals robust demand conditions, but triggers negative S&P 500 returns in late-cycle expansions due to overheating concerns and policy tightening expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation as measured by the CPI signals accommodative monetary policy, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns through lower real interest rates and improved risk appetite.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains below 2% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production below 95 over two months signals weakening manufacturing activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns as recession risks escalate.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below 95 over 2 months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and payrolls signal weak demand conditions, creating negative S&P 500 returns as both consumer and business spending contract.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the combined HOUST-PAYEMS signal falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment rates above 7% signal a slack labor market, leading to negative S&P 500 returns as corporate profit margins face downward pressure from wage stagnation.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7%.
